scope.summary: Implement FBX animated garment export with joint-animation auto-key bake: for each frame in the export range, write a joint transform keyframe even on frames with no authored key, so the exported FBX is a fully-baked dense curve that downstream DCC tools can import without drift.
acceptance_criteria: FBX export path exists in tailor-solver/src/export/fbx.rs and is wired into the animation export API alongside glTF/Alembic/OBJ-sequence.
The exporter bakes joint transforms at every frame in the export range (auto-key), not only at frames that have an authored keyframe in the Tailor timeline.

A garment animated with 3 sparse avatar-pose keyframes exports an FBX with N dense joint-transform keys (one per frame), and reimporting that FBX into a reference DCC reconstructs the same pose curve within float32 tolerance.
The export range parameters from the Animation Range Export MT apply to FBX export.
scope.risk_if_missed: FBX handoff to Maya, Blender, and UE with sparse keyframe sources produces drifting or snapping joint animation in the downstream DCC. MD 2026.0 auto-keys are the standard industry expectation for FBX cloth animation delivery. Without them Tailor's FBX output is rejected or produces visible animation artifacts in production.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
